Specimen information

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high chloride. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c with normal glucose may indicate recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Findings are consistent with possible acute or chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
